title: M09 Mastery Check — TypeScript Advanced
description: Quick applied check. No looking things up. Answer from memory.

questions.prompt: Create a type for a PATCH /users/:id endpoint body — all User fields optional.
Design a discriminated union for a network request state with loading/success/error states.
questions.answer_key: type State<T> = { status: 'loading' } | { status: 'success'; data: T } | { status: 'error'; message: string };

questions.prompt: ReturnType<typeof fn> extracts the return type of fn at compile time.
questions.explanation: ReturnType<F> uses TypeScript's infer keyword under the hood to extract the return type of function type F.

questions.prompt: How do you make TypeScript error if a new shape is added to a discriminated union but not handled in a switch?
questions.answer_key: Add default: const _: never = shape; — if any unhandled member reaches default, it's not assignable to never, causing a compile error.
